scenario: Full breakout storm: the agent combines recon, bot evasion and AI injection with high-volume RCE (Log4Shell, Spring4Shell, Struts), SSRF to the cloud metadata endpoint (169.254.169.254), and path traversal across every SoleDrop endpoint at once.

rules.name: SoleDrop-CTF-Box4-AgenticBreakout
mitre: T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application (Log4Shell CVE-2021-44228, Spring4Shell CVE-2022-22965, Struts CVE-2017-5638)
atlas: AML.T0040 ML Model Inference API Access
rules.primary_signal: Same actor (entity) shows recon/injection activity AND high-confidence RCE/SSRF/traversal exploitation in one window — the escalation from probing to breakout.
rules.correlation_design: Two sub-queries, matchInOrder=false (the storm is concurrent, not strictly ordered). entity = 'ip' (the source IP is the actor key Cloudflare provides; see entity note). SubQuery 1 = recon OR prompt-injection markers (the lead-in). SubQuery 2 = RCE/SSRF/traversal exploitation markers (the breakout). Both must hit in the 30-min window for the same entity.

ocsf_fields.http_request.user_agent: mapped — JNDI-in-UA (the attack puts Log4Shell in the UA)

rules.entity_note: entity must be a tenant-supported correlation entity. 'user' is tenant-confirmed; IP/device-style entities also exist (per detection-rule-types.md) but the exact identifier string for an IP entity is TO-VERIFY on the live tenant. If an IP entity is not available, deploy the scheduled variant below (SoleDrop-CTF-Box4-MultiVectorStorm), which groups by src_endpoint.ip directly and needs no correlation-entity support.

hunt_query.powerquery_recon_leg: dataSource.name='Cloudflare' class_uid=4002 http_request.url.hostname contains '.lab.soledrop.co' | filter (http_request.url.path in ('/.env','/.git/HEAD','/api/v1/admin','/api/v1/training-data','/actuator/env') OR http_request.url.url_string matches '(?i)(ignore (all )?previous instructions|do anything now|\bDAN\b|jailbreak)') | group recon_hits=count(), last=newest(timestamp) by src_endpoint.ip | sort -recon_hits | limit 100
hunt_query.powerquery_breakout_leg: dataSource.name='Cloudflare' class_uid=4002 http_request.url.hostname contains '.lab.soledrop.co' | filter (http_request.url.url_string matches '(?i)(\$\{jndi:|\$\{\$\{|ognl|classLoader|\.\./\.\./|%2e%2e%2f|169\.254\.169\.254|metadata\.google\.internal|file:///etc/passwd)' OR http_request.user_agent matches '(?i)(\$\{jndi:|\$\{\$\{|\$\{lower:|\$\{::)') | group rce_hits=count(), last=newest(timestamp), uri=any(http_request.url.url_string), ua=any(http_request.user_agent) by src_endpoint.ip | sort -rce_hits | limit 100
hunt_query.note: Run each leg standalone via powerquery_run to confirm both produce rows for the CTF source IP during the Box 4 storm. The correlation rule below uses the boolean (no-pipe) S1QL forms of these two filters as sub-queries.

data.name: SoleDrop-CTF-Box4-AgenticBreakout
data.description: Agentic multi-vector breakout: same source shows recon/prompt-injection AND RCE/SSRF/traversal exploitation in 30 min. Log4Shell/Spring4Shell/Struts + SSRF to 169.254.169.254 + path traversal. MITRE T1190 / T1119 / T1020; ATLAS AML.T0054 / AML.T0040. Source: Cloudflare class_uid=4002.

subQueries.subQuery: dataSource.name = 'Cloudflare' AND class_uid = 4002 AND (http_request.url.path in ('/.env','/.git/HEAD','/api/v1/admin','/api/v1/training-data','/actuator/env') OR http_request.url.url_string matches '(?i)(ignore (all )?previous instructions|do anything now|\bDAN\b|jailbreak)')

dataSource.name = 'Cloudflare' AND class_uid = 4002 AND (http_request.url.url_string matches '(?i)(\$\{jndi:|\$\{\$\{|ognl|classLoader|%2e%2e%2f|169\.254\.169\.254|metadata\.google\.internal|file:///etc/passwd)' OR http_request.user_agent matches '(?i)(\$\{jndi:|\$\{lower:|\$\{::)')

rules.api_body_note: entity='ip' is TO-VERIFY — confirm the tenant's supported IP correlation-entity identifier (detection-rule-types.md confirms 'user'; device/IP entities exist but the exact token varies by tenant). If 'ip' is rejected, either substitute the confirmed IP-entity token or fall back to the scheduled MultiVectorStorm rule. ESCAPING: both subQuery regexes hold single backslashes after JSON-parsing this file; the HTTP-client serializer re-doubles them on the wire so the engine receives one. Do NOT hand-add extra backslashes. The X-Forwarded-For spoofing in the attack means ClientIP (src_endpoint.ip) is the REAL origin, NOT the spoofed 185.220.x.x header — correlating on src_endpoint.ip is correct.
rules.asset_binding: Correlation rule: auto-binds from matched events, but Cloudflare events need device.uid + class_uid (asset enrichment) to bind a real device; else 'Unknown Device'. entityMappings optional for correlation; the IP entity is the actor key.
rules.deploy: POST -> enable {"filter":{"ids":["<id>"]}} -> poll ?ids=<id>&isLegacy=false until Active. Correlation rules evaluate streaming once thresholds met in the window.

rules.name: SoleDrop-CTF-Box4-MultiVectorStorm
atlas: AML.T0024 Exfiltration via ML Inference API
rules.primary_signal: One src_endpoint.ip firing MULTIPLE distinct attack classes (RCE + SSRF + traversal + injection) in the window — breadth of attack types from a single source = coordinated breakout. Also the deploy-safe alternative to the correlation rule when IP-entity is unavailable.

ocsf_fields.unmapped.WAFSQLiAttackScore / unmapped.W: mapped (str) — wrap number(); LOWER = WORSE (live-verified: confirmed attacks scored 1-10, clean traffic ~97); corroboration
ocsf_fields.tls.ja3_hash.value: mapped (JA3Hash) — chain pivot (use in place of JA4, which is not PQ-addressable)
ocsf_fields.unmapped.WAFRCEAttackScore: mapped (str, wrap number()) — confirmed live, LOWER = WORSE
ocsf_fields.unmapped.WAFSQLiAttackScore: mapped (str, wrap number()) — confirmed live, LOWER = WORSE
ocsf_fields.unmapped.WAFXSSAttackScore: mapped, same family as SQLi/RCE (inferred, not separately fired in validation)

hunt_query.powerquery: dataSource.name='Cloudflare' class_uid=4002 http_request.url.hostname contains '.lab.soledrop.co' | let payload = http_request.url.url_string + ' ' + http_request.user_agent | let is_rce = if(payload matches '(?i)(\$\{jndi:|\$\{lower:|ognl|classLoader|\$\{::)', 1, 0) | let is_ssrf = if(payload matches '(?i)(169\.254\.169\.254|metadata\.google\.internal|file:///)', 1, 0) | let is_trav = if(payload matches '(?i)(\.\./\.\./|%2e%2e%2f|/etc/passwd|/etc/shadow)', 1, 0) | let is_inj = if(payload matches '(?i)(ignore (all )?previous instructions|do anything now|\bDAN\b|jailbreak|union select|or 1=1)', 1, 0) | filter (is_rce + is_ssrf + is_trav + is_inj) > 0 | group rce=max(is_rce), ssrf=max(is_ssrf), trav=max(is_trav), inj=max(is_inj), hits=count(), worst_sqli=min(number(unmapped.WAFSQLiAttackScore)), worst_rce=min(number(unmapped.WAFRCEAttackScore)), first_seen=oldest(timestamp), last_seen=newest(timestamp), ja3=any(tls.ja3_hash.value) by src_ip=src_endpoint.ip | let attack_classes = rce + ssrf + trav + inj | filter attack_classes >= 2 | columns src_ip, attack_classes, rce, ssrf, trav, inj, hits, worst_sqli, worst_rce, ja3, first_seen, last_seen | sort -attack_classes | limit 100
hunt_query.note: attack_classes >= 2 = a source firing at least two distinct exploitation categories. The full breakout storm trips all four. CORRECTED (live-verified): the per-class unmapped.WAFSQLiAttackScore/WAFRCEAttackScore fields ARE confirmed and populated as strings; wrap in number() and remember LOWER = WORSE (score 1-10 on confirmed attacks, ~97 on clean traffic) — the opposite of the >=90 guess this note originally suggested. Payload-regex classifiers (is_rce/is_ssrf/is_trav/is_inj) are kept as the primary signal since they classify the exploit TYPE; the WAF scores serve as corroboration.
hunt_query.purple_ai_equivalent: Find requests in the last 15 minutes where WAFRCEAttackScore > 90 AND BotDetectionTags contains python AND ClientRequestPath contains training-data; show RayID, payload, JA4.

data.name: SoleDrop-CTF-Box4-MultiVectorStorm
data.description: One source firing >=2 distinct attack classes (RCE/SSRF/traversal/injection) on SoleDrop in the window = coordinated agentic breakout. Log4Shell/Spring4Shell/Struts + SSRF 169.254.169.254 + traversal. MITRE T1190 / T1213 / T1119; ATLAS AML.T0054 / AML.T0024. Source: Cloudflare class_uid=4002.

scheduledParams.query: dataSource.name='Cloudflare' class_uid=4002 http_request.url.hostname contains '.lab.soledrop.co' | let payload = http_request.url.url_string + ' ' + http_request.user_agent | let is_rce = if(payload matches '(?i)(\$\{jndi:|\$\{lower:|ognl|classLoader|\$\{::)', 1, 0) | let is_ssrf = if(payload matches '(?i)(169\.254\.169\.254|metadata\.google\.internal|file:///)', 1, 0) | let is_trav = if(payload matches '(?i)(\.\./\.\./|%2e%2e%2f|/etc/passwd|/etc/shadow)', 1, 0) | let is_inj = if(payload matches '(?i)(ignore (all )?previous instructions|do anything now|\bDAN\b|jailbreak|union select|or 1=1)', 1, 0) | filter (is_rce + is_ssrf + is_trav + is_inj) > 0 | group rce=max(is_rce), ssrf=max(is_ssrf), trav=max(is_trav), inj=max(is_inj), hits=count(), worst_sqli=min(number(unmapped.WAFSQLiAttackScore)), worst_rce=min(number(unmapped.WAFRCEAttackScore)), first_seen=oldest(timestamp), last_seen=newest(timestamp), ja3=any(tls.ja3_hash.value) by src_ip=src_endpoint.ip | let attack_classes = rce + ssrf + trav + inj | filter attack_classes >= 2 | columns src_ip, attack_classes, rce, ssrf, trav, inj, hits, worst_sqli, worst_rce, ja3, first_seen, last_seen | sort -attack_classes | limit 100

rules.api_body_note: Verify `if(...)` and string concatenation (`+`) syntax against the live functions-reference before deploy — if `if()` is unsupported in the tenant's PQ dialect, replace each is_* with a separate filtered count via grouped CASE or split into per-class sub-aggregations. ESCAPING: scheduledParams.query holds single backslashes after JSON-parsing this file; the HTTP-client serializer re-doubles them on the wire. Do NOT hand-add extra backslashes.
rules.asset_binding: Scheduled rule: entityMappings on src_ip + ja4. Device = 'Unknown Device' until asset enrichment; src_ip + ja4 drive the critical HA SOAR playbook (block IP + JA4 in CF, isolate injected tenants, revoke keys).
rules.deploy: POST -> enable -> poll until Active -> wait one 15-min interval. Scheduled-rule constraints apply. List with isLegacy=false.
